summary_zh: 当一个智能体延迟时，执行多智能体计划可能会面临挑战，因为这通常会导致与其他智能体发生冲突。因此，我们需要快速找到一个新的安全计划。仅对延迟的智能体进行重规划通常无法产生高效的计划，有时甚至无法产生可行的计划。另一方面，对其他智能体进行重规划可能会导致级联的变化和延迟。我们展示了如何通过跟踪和利用其他智能体的时间灵活性来高效地进行重规划，同时避免级联延迟。这种灵活性是指智能体在不改变其他更多智能体的顺序或进一步延迟它们的情况下可以承受的最大延迟。我们的算法 FlexSIPP 预先计算延迟智能体的所有可能计划，并且返回给定场景中任何单个智能体延迟时，其他智能体的更改。我们在一个实际案例研究中展示了我们的方法，即在高度密集的荷兰铁路网络中对火车进行重规划。我们的实验表明，FlexSIPP 提供了有效的解决方案，与现实世界的调整相关，并且在合理的时间范围内完成。

title: Personalized Model-Based Design of Human Centric AI enabled CPS for Long term usage
summary: Human centric critical systems are increasingly involving artificial intelligence to enable knowledge extraction from sensor collected data. Examples include medical monitoring and control systems, gesture based human computer interaction systems, and autonomous cars. Such systems are intended to operate for a long term potentially for a lifetime in many scenarios such as closed loop blood glucose control for Type 1 diabetics, self-driving cars, and monitoting systems for stroke diagnosis, and rehabilitation. Long term operation of such AI enabled human centric applications can expose them to corner cases for which their operation is may be uncertain. This can be due to many reasons such as inherent flaws in the design, limited resources for testing, inherent computational limitations of the testing methodology, or unknown use cases resulting from human interaction with the system. Such untested corner cases or cases for which the system performance is uncertain can lead to violations in the safety, sustainability, and security requirements of the system. In this paper, we analyze the existing techniques for safety, sustainability, and security analysis of an AI enabled human centric control system and discuss their limitations for testing the system for long term use in practice. We then propose personalized model based solutions for potentially eliminating such limitations.
tldr: This paper discusses the limitations of current safety, sustainability, and security analysis techniques for long-term AI-enabled human-centric systems and proposes personalized model-based solutions to address these limitations.
tldr_zh: 该论文讨论了当前针对长期人工智能赋能的人为中心系统的安全性、可持续性和安全性分析技术的局限性，并提出了个性化的基于模型解决方案以解决这些问题。
summary_zh: 以人为中心的关键系统越来越多地采用人工智能技术，以实现从传感器收集的数据中提取知识。例如，医疗监控和控制系统、基于手势的人机交互系统和自动驾驶汽车。此类系统旨在长期运行，在许多场景中甚至可能终身运行，例如针对1型糖尿病患者的闭环血糖控制、自动驾驶汽车以及用于中风诊断和康复的监控系统。基于人工智能的以人为中心的应用的长期运行可能会使其暴露于边缘案例，在这些案例中，其运行可能是不确定的。这可能是由于多种原因造成的，例如设计中固有的缺陷、有限的测试资源、测试方法固有的计算限制，或由于人与系统交互而产生的未知用例。这些未经测试的边缘案例或系统性能不确定的案例可能会导致系统在安全性、可持续性和安全方面的要求受到违反。在本文中，我们分析了现有的针对基于人工智能的以人为中心控制系统的安全性、可持续性和安全性分析技术，并讨论了它们在测试系统以供长期实际使用方面的局限性。然后，我们提出了基于个性化模型的解决方案，以潜在地消除这些局限性。

title: Paradoxical noise preference in RNNs
summary: In recurrent neural networks (RNNs) used to model biological neural networks, noise is typically introduced during training to emulate biological variability and regularize learning. The expectation is that removing the noise at test time should preserve or improve performance. Contrary to this intuition, we find that continuous-time recurrent neural networks (CTRNNs) often perform best at a nonzero noise level, specifically, the same level used during training. This noise preference typically arises when noise is injected inside the neural activation function; networks trained with noise injected outside the activation function perform best with zero noise. Through analyses of simple function approximation, maze navigation, and single neuron regulator tasks, we show that the phenomenon stems from noise-induced shifts of fixed points (stationary distributions) in the underlying stochastic dynamics of the RNNs. These fixed point shifts are noise-level dependent and bias the network outputs when the noise is removed, degrading performance. Analytical and numerical results show that the bias arises when neural states operate near activation function nonlinearities, where noise is asymmetrically attenuated, and that performance optimization incentivizes operation near these nonlinearities. Thus, networks can overfit to the stochastic training environment itself rather than just to the input-output data. The phenomenon is distinct from stochastic resonance, wherein nonzero noise enhances signal processing. Our findings reveal that training noise can become an integral part of the computation learned by recurrent networks, with implications for understanding neural population dynamics and for the design of robust artificial RNNs.
tldr: The paper demonstrates that recurrent neural networks (RNNs) can exhibit paradoxical noise preference, where performance is optimal at a non-zero noise level matching the training noise. This is attributed to noise-induced fixed point shifts and overfitting to the stochastic training environment.
tldr_zh: 该论文表明，循环神经网络(RNNs)可能表现出反常的噪声偏好，即在与训练噪声相匹配的非零噪声水平下，性能达到最佳。这归因于噪声引起的固定点漂移以及对随机训练环境的过度拟合。
summary_zh: 在用于模拟生物神经网络的循环神经网络（RNN）中，通常在训练期间引入噪声，以模拟生物变异并正则化学习。人们期望在测试时消除噪声应保持或提高性能。与这种直觉相反，我们发现连续时间循环神经网络（CTRNN）通常在非零噪声水平下表现最佳，具体来说，是训练期间使用的相同水平。这种噪声偏好通常出现在噪声被注入到神经元激活函数内部时；使用注入在激活函数外部的噪声训练的网络在零噪声下表现最佳。通过对简单函数逼近、迷宫导航和单神经元调节器任务的分析，我们表明这种现象源于RNN底层随机动力学中不动点（平稳分布）的噪声诱导偏移。这些不动点偏移与噪声水平相关，并在噪声消除时偏置网络输出，从而降低性能。分析和数值结果表明，当神经元状态在激活函数的非线性区域附近运行时，会产生偏差，此时噪声被不对称地衰减，并且性能优化激励在这些非线性区域附近运行。因此，网络可能会过度拟合到随机训练环境本身，而不仅仅是输入-输出数据。这种现象与随机共振不同，后者的非零噪声增强了信号处理。我们的发现表明，训练噪声可能成为循环网络学习到的计算不可分割的一部分，这对理解神经种群动力学和设计鲁棒的人工RNN具有重要意义。

title: TSSR: Two-Stage Swap-Reward-Driven Reinforcement Learning for Character-Level SMILES Generation
summary: The design of reliable, valid, and diverse molecules is fundamental to modern drug discovery, as improved molecular generation supports efficient exploration of the chemical space for potential drug candidates and reduces the cost of early design efforts. Despite these needs, current chemical language models that generate molecules as SMILES strings are vulnerable to compounding token errors: many samples are unparseable or chemically implausible, and hard constraints meant to prevent failure can restrict exploration. To address this gap, we introduce TSSR, a Two-Stage, Swap-Reward-driven reinforcement learning (RL) framework for character-level SMILES generation. Stage one rewards local token swaps that repair syntax, promoting transitions from invalid to parseable strings. Stage two provides chemistry-aware feedback from RDKit diagnostics, rewarding reductions in valence, aromaticity, and connectivity issues. The reward decomposes into interpretable terms (swap efficiency, error reduction, distance to validity), is model agnostic, and requires no task-specific labels or hand-crafted grammars. We evaluated TSSR on the MOSES benchmark using a GRU policy trained with PPO in both pure RL (P-RL) from random initialization and fine-tuning RL (F-RL) starting from a pretrained chemical language model, assessing 10,000 generated SMILES per run. In P-RL, TSSR significantly improves syntactic validity, chemical validity, and novelty. In F-RL, TSSR preserves drug-likeness and synthesizability while increasing validity and novelty. Token-level analysis shows that syntax edits and chemistry fixes act jointly to reduce RDKit detected errors. TSSR converts a sparse terminal objective into a denser and more interpretable reward, improving both syntactic and chemical quality without reducing diversity. TSSR is dataset-agnostic and can be adapted to various reinforcement learning approaches.
tldr: The paper introduces TSSR, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework using token swaps and RDKit diagnostics to improve the validity and novelty of generated SMILES strings for drug discovery, outperforming existing methods. It addresses problems with chemical language models by introducing reward decomposing and error reduction mechanisms.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 TSSR，一个两阶段强化学习框架，使用 token 交换和 RDKit 诊断来提高生成 SMILES 字符串的有效性和新颖性，优于现有方法。它通过引入奖励分解和减少错误机制来解决化学语言模型的问题。
summary_zh: 可靠、有效且多样化的分子设计是现代药物发现的基础，因为改进的分子生成有助于高效探索化学空间以寻找潜在的候选药物，并降低早期设计工作的成本。尽管有这些需求，但当前将分子生成为SMILES字符串的化学语言模型容易出现复合式的token错误：许多样本无法解析或在化学上不可信，而旨在防止失败的硬约束可能会限制探索。为了解决这一差距，我们引入了TSSR，一个用于字符级SMILES生成的双阶段、交换奖励驱动的强化学习（RL）框架。第一阶段奖励修复语法的局部token交换，从而促进从无效字符串到可解析字符串的转换。第二阶段提供来自RDKit诊断程序的化学感知反馈，奖励效价、芳香性和连接性问题的减少。该奖励分解为可解释的项（交换效率、错误减少、与有效性的距离），与模型无关，并且不需要特定于任务的标签或手工制作的语法。我们使用在MOSES基准上进行评估，使用在纯强化学习（P-RL）中从随机初始化训练以及在从预训练化学语言模型开始进行微调强化学习（F-RL）中训练的PPO GRU策略，每次运行评估10,000个生成的SMILES。在P-RL中，TSSR显著提高了句法有效性、化学有效性和新颖性。在F-RL中，TSSR在提高有效性和新颖性的同时，保持了类药性和可合成性。Token级别的分析表明，语法编辑和化学修复共同作用以减少RDKit检测到的错误。TSSR将稀疏的终端目标转化为更密集且更可解释的奖励，从而在不降低多样性的前提下，提高句法和化学质量。TSSR与数据集无关，可以适应各种强化学习方法。

title: Higher-Order Adversarial Patches for Real-Time Object Detectors
summary: Higher-order adversarial attacks can directly be considered the result of a cat-and-mouse game -- an elaborate action involving constant pursuit, near captures, and repeated escapes. This idiom describes the enduring circular training of adversarial attack patterns and adversarial training the best. The following work investigates the impact of higher-order adversarial attacks on object detectors by successively training attack patterns and hardening object detectors with adversarial training. The YOLOv10 object detector is chosen as a representative, and adversarial patches are used in an evasion attack manner. Our results indicate that higher-order adversarial patches are not only affecting the object detector directly trained on but rather provide a stronger generalization capacity compared to lower-order adversarial patches. Moreover, the results highlight that solely adversarial training is not sufficient to harden an object detector efficiently against this kind of adversarial attack. Code: https://github.com/JensBayer/HigherOrder
tldr: This paper explores the effectiveness of higher-order adversarial patches in evading YOLOv10 object detectors and finds they generalize better than lower-order patches while highlighting the limitations of standard adversarial training against such attacks.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了高阶对抗补丁在规避 YOLOv10 目标检测器方面的有效性，发现它们比低阶补丁具有更好的泛化能力，并强调了标准对抗训练在对抗此类攻击方面的局限性。
summary_zh: 高阶对抗攻击可以直接被视为一种猫鼠游戏的结果——一种涉及持续追踪、险些捕获和反复逃脱的精心行动。这个习语最能描述对抗攻击模式和对抗训练两者之间持久的循环训练。本文研究了高阶对抗攻击对目标检测器的影响，方法是通过连续训练攻击模式并使用对抗训练来强化目标检测器。我们选择YOLOv10目标检测器作为代表，并以规避攻击的方式使用对抗补丁。我们的结果表明，高阶对抗补丁不仅影响直接训练的目标检测器，而且与低阶对抗补丁相比，提供了更强的泛化能力。此外，结果还强调仅靠对抗训练不足以有效地强化目标检测器，使其免受此类对抗攻击。代码：https://github.com/JensBayer/HigherOrder

title: OceanSplat: Object-aware Gaussian Splatting with Trinocular View Consistency for Underwater Scene Reconstruction
summary: We introduce OceanSplat, a novel 3D Gaussian Splatting-based approach for accurately representing 3D geometry in underwater scenes. To overcome multi-view inconsistencies caused by underwater optical degradation, our method enforces trinocular view consistency by rendering horizontally and vertically translated camera views relative to each input view and aligning them via inverse warping. Furthermore, these translated camera views are used to derive a synthetic epipolar depth prior through triangulation, which serves as a self-supervised depth regularizer. These geometric constraints facilitate the spatial optimization of 3D Gaussians and preserve scene structure in underwater environments. We also propose a depth-aware alpha adjustment that modulates the opacity of 3D Gaussians during early training based on their $z$-component and viewing direction, deterring the formation of medium-induced primitives. With our contributions, 3D Gaussians are disentangled from the scattering medium, enabling robust representation of object geometry and significantly reducing floating artifacts in reconstructed underwater scenes. Experiments on real-world underwater and simulated scenes demonstrate that OceanSplat substantially outperforms existing methods for both scene reconstruction and restoration in scattering media.
tldr: OceanSplat introduces a novel Gaussian Splatting approach for underwater scene reconstruction, addressing inconsistencies caused by optical degradation via trinocular view consistency and a synthetic epipolar depth prior.
tldr_zh: OceanSplat 提出了一种新的基于高斯溅射的水下场景重建方法，通过三目视图一致性和合成的极线深度先验来解决光学退化引起的不一致性。
summary_zh: 我们介绍 OceanSplat，一种新颖的基于 3D 高斯 Splatting 的方法，用于精确地表示水下场景中的 3D 几何结构。为了克服水下光学退化导致的多视图不一致性，我们的方法通过渲染相对于每个输入视图水平和垂直平移的相机视图，并通过逆向扭曲对齐它们，从而强制执行三目视图一致性。此外，这些平移后的相机视图被用来通过三角测量导出合成的极线深度先验，作为自监督深度正则化器。这些几何约束有助于 3D 高斯体的空间优化，并在水下环境中保持场景结构。我们还提出了一种深度感知的 Alpha 调整，该调整在早期训练期间基于 3D 高斯体的 $z$ 分量和视角方向来调节 3D 高斯体的不透明度，从而阻止介质诱导的基元的形成。通过我们的贡献，3D 高斯体与散射介质分离，从而能够稳健地表示物体几何结构，并显著减少重建的水下场景中的漂浮伪影。在真实水下和模拟场景上的实验表明，OceanSplat 在散射介质中的场景重建和恢复方面，显著优于现有方法。

title: SparseLaneSTP: Leveraging Spatio-Temporal Priors with Sparse Transformers for 3D Lane Detection
summary: 3D lane detection has emerged as a critical challenge in autonomous driving, encompassing identification and localization of lane markings and the 3D road surface. Conventional 3D methods detect lanes from dense birds-eye-viewed (BEV) features, though erroneous transformations often result in a poor feature representation misaligned with the true 3D road surface. While recent sparse lane detectors have surpassed dense BEV approaches, they completely disregard valuable lane-specific priors. Furthermore, existing methods fail to utilize historic lane observations, which yield the potential to resolve ambiguities in situations of poor visibility. To address these challenges, we present SparseLaneSTP, a novel method that integrates both geometric properties of the lane structure and temporal information into a sparse lane transformer. It introduces a new lane-specific spatio-temporal attention mechanism, a continuous lane representation tailored for sparse architectures as well as temporal regularization. Identifying weaknesses of existing 3D lane datasets, we also introduce a precise and consistent 3D lane dataset using a simple yet effective auto-labeling strategy. Our experimental section proves the benefits of our contributions and demonstrates state-of-the-art performance across all detection and error metrics on existing 3D lane detection benchmarks as well as on our novel dataset.
tldr: This paper introduces SparseLaneSTP, a novel 3D lane detection method using sparse transformers with spatio-temporal priors. It also introduces a new 3D lane dataset with an auto-labeling strategy.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SparseLaneSTP，一种使用具有时空先验的稀疏Transformer的新型3D车道检测方法。它还引入了一个使用自动标注策略的新的3D车道数据集。
summary_zh: 3D车道线检测已成为自动驾驶领域的一项关键挑战，其包括车道线标记和3D路面识别与定位。传统的3D方法通常从密集的鸟瞰图（BEV）特征中检测车道线，但错误的变换往往导致特征表示不良，与真实的3D路面错位。虽然最近的稀疏车道线检测器已经超越了密集的BEV方法，但它们完全忽略了有价值的车道线特有先验知识。此外，现有方法未能利用历史车道线观测，这能够解决在低能见度情况下的歧义性。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出SparseLaneSTP，一种将车道线结构的几何属性和时间信息集成到稀疏车道线Transformer中的新方法。它引入了一种新的车道线特有的时空注意力机制、一种为稀疏架构定制化的连续车道线表示以及时间正则化。针对现有3D车道线数据集的缺陷，我们还引入了一种使用简单而有效的自动标注策略构建的精确且一致的3D车道线数据集。我们的实验部分证明了我们贡献的优势，并展示了在现有3D车道线检测基准以及我们新数据集上的所有检测和误差指标方面的最先进性能。

title: DivAS: Interactive 3D Segmentation of NeRFs via Depth-Weighted Voxel Aggregation
summary: Existing methods for segmenting Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) are often optimization-based, requiring slow per-scene training that sacrifices the zero-shot capabilities of 2D foundation models. We introduce DivAS (Depth-interactive Voxel Aggregation Segmentation), an optimization-free, fully interactive framework that addresses these limitations. Our method operates via a fast GUI-based workflow where 2D SAM masks, generated from user point prompts, are refined using NeRF-derived depth priors to improve geometric accuracy and foreground-background separation. The core of our contribution is a custom CUDA kernel that aggregates these refined multi-view masks into a unified 3D voxel grid in under 200ms, enabling real-time visual feedback. This optimization-free design eliminates the need for per-scene training. Experiments on Mip-NeRF 360° and LLFF show that DivAS achieves segmentation quality comparable to optimization-based methods, while being 2-2.5x faster end-to-end, and up to an order of magnitude faster when excluding user prompting time.
tldr: The paper introduces DivAS, a novel, optimization-free interactive 3D segmentation framework for NeRFs using depth-weighted voxel aggregation achieving comparable segmentation quality to optimization-based methods with significant speed improvements.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DivAS，一种新颖的、无需优化的交互式NeRFs 3D分割框架，通过深度加权体素聚合实现。该方法在显著提高速度的同时，达到了与基于优化的方法相当的分割质量。
summary_zh: 用于分割神经辐射场（NeRFs）的现有方法通常基于优化，需要缓慢的单场景训练，从而牺牲了2D基础模型的零样本能力。我们提出了DivAS（深度交互体素聚合分割），一个无需优化、完全交互式的框架，旨在解决这些局限性。我们的方法通过快速的基于GUI的工作流程运作，其中从用户点提示生成的2D SAM掩码，通过使用NeRF衍生的深度先验进行细化，以提高几何精度和前景-背景分离。我们的核心贡献是一个定制的CUDA内核，可在200毫秒内将这些精细化的多视角掩码聚合到一个统一的3D体素网格中，从而实现实时的视觉反馈。这种无需优化的设计消除了对单场景训练的需求。在Mip-NeRF 360°和LLFF上的实验表明，DivAS的分割质量可与基于优化的方法相媲美，同时端到端的速度快2-2.5倍，如果在不包括用户提示时间的情况下，速度可快一个数量级。

title: Detection of Deployment Operational Deviations for Safety and Security of AI-Enabled Human-Centric Cyber Physical Systems
summary:
In recent years, Human-centric cyber-physical systems have increasingly involved artificial intelligence to enable knowledge extraction from sensor-collected data. Examples include medical monitoring and control systems, as well as autonomous cars. Such systems are intended to operate according to the protocols and guidelines for regular system operations. However, in many scenarios, such as closed-loop blood glucose control for Type 1 diabetics, self-driving cars, and monitoring systems for stroke diagnosis. The operations of such AI-enabled human-centric applications can expose them to cases for which their operational mode may be uncertain, for instance, resulting from the interactions with a human with the system. Such cases, in which the system is in uncertain conditions, can violate the system's safety and security requirements. 
  This paper will discuss operational deviations that can lead these systems to operate in unknown conditions. We will then create a framework to evaluate different strategies for ensuring the safety and security of AI-enabled human-centric cyber-physical systems in operation deployment. Then, as an example, we show a personalized image-based novel technique for detecting the non-announcement of meals in closed-loop blood glucose control for Type 1 diabetics.
tldr: This paper proposes a framework to detect operational deviations in AI-enabled human-centric cyber-physical systems, exemplified by a novel technique for meal detection in closed-loop blood glucose control.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种框架，用于检测人工智能人本网络物理系统中操作偏差，并以闭环血糖控制中膳食检测的新技术为例。
summary_zh:
近年来，以人为中心的网络物理系统越来越多地采用人工智能技术，以便从传感器收集的数据中提取知识。例如，医疗监控和控制系统以及自动驾驶汽车。这些系统旨在根据常规系统操作的协议和指南运行。然而，在许多场景中，例如1型糖尿病的闭环血糖控制、自动驾驶汽车以及卒中诊断的监控系统，这些AI驱动的以人为中心的应用程序的操作可能会使其面临工作模式不确定的情况，例如由于与系统中的人交互而导致的情况。这些系统处于不确定条件下的情况可能会违反系统的安全和保障要求。

本文将探讨可能导致这些系统在未知条件下运行的操作偏差。然后，我们将创建一个框架来评估在运营部署中确保AI驱动的以人为中心的网络物理系统的安全和保障的不同策略。然后，作为一个例子，我们展示了一种个性化的基于图像的新型技术，用于检测1型糖尿病闭环血糖控制中未宣布的用餐。

title: A zone-based training approach for last-mile routing using Graph Neural Networks and Pointer Networks
summary:
Rapid e-commerce growth has pushed last-mile delivery networks to their limits, where small routing gains translate into lower costs, faster service, and fewer emissions. Classical heuristics struggle to adapt when travel times are highly asymmetric (e.g., one-way streets, congestion). A deep learning-based approach to the last-mile routing problem is presented to generate geographical zones composed of stop sequences to minimize last-mile delivery times.
  The presented approach is an encoder-decoder architecture. Each route is represented as a complete directed graph whose nodes are stops and whose edge weights are asymmetric travel times. A Graph Neural Network encoder produces node embeddings that captures the spatial relationships between stops. A Pointer Network decoder then takes the embeddings and the route's start node to sequentially select the next stops, assigning a probability to each unvisited node as the next destination.
  Cells of a Discrete Global Grid System which contain route stops in the training data are obtained and clustered to generate geographical zones of similar size in which the process of training and inference are divided. Subsequently, a different instance of the model is trained per zone only considering the stops of the training routes which are included in that zone.
  This approach is evaluated using the Los Angeles routes from the 2021 Amazon Last Mile Routing Challenge. Results from general and zone-based training are compared, showing a reduction in the average predicted route length in the zone-based training compared to the general training. The performance improvement of the zone-based approach becomes more pronounced as the number of stops per route increases.
tldr: This paper presents a zone-based training approach using Graph Neural Networks and Pointer Networks for last-mile routing, showing improved route length prediction on the Amazon Last Mile Routing Challenge dataset compared to general training.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于区域的训练方法，使用图神经网络和指针网络进行最后一英里路线规划。结果表明，与一般训练相比，该方法在 Amazon Last Mile Routing Challenge 数据集上提高了路线长度预测的准确性。
summary_zh:
快速增长的电子商务已经将末端配送网络推向极限，在此背景下，小幅的路径优化都可能转化为更低的成本、更快的服务和更少的排放。当通行时间高度不对称（例如，单行道、拥堵）时，经典的启发式算法难以适应。本文提出了一种基于深度学习的末端路由问题解决方法，该方法生成由停靠点序列组成地理区域，以最大限度地减少末端配送时间。

提出的方法是一种编码器-解码器架构。每条路径都被表示为一个完整的有向图，其节点为停靠点，边权重为不对称通行时间。图神经网络编码器产生节点嵌入，捕捉停靠点之间的空间关系。然后，指针网络解码器利用这些嵌入和路径的起始节点，顺序地选择下一个停靠点，为每个未访问的节点分配一个作为下一个目的地的概率。

获取训练数据中包含路径停靠点的离散全局网格系统的单元格，并通过聚类生成大小相似的地理区域，并在这些区域中进行训练和推理。 随后，针对每个区域训练一个不同的模型实例，仅考虑包含在该区域的训练路径的停靠点。

该方法使用2021年亚马逊末端路由挑战赛中的洛杉矶路径数据进行评估。对通用训练和基于区域的训练结果进行了比较，显示基于区域的训练相比于通用训练，平均预测路径长度有所减少。随着每条路径停靠点数量的增加，基于区域的方法的性能改进变得更加显著。

title: Timeliness-Oriented Scheduling and Resource Allocation in Multi-Region Collaborative Perception
summary: Collaborative perception (CP) is a critical technology in applications like autonomous driving and smart cities. It involves the sharing and fusion of information among sensors to overcome the limitations of individual perception, such as blind spots and range limitations. However, CP faces two primary challenges. First, due to the dynamic nature of the environment, the timeliness of the transmitted information is critical to perception performance. Second, with limited computational power at the sensors and constrained wireless bandwidth, the communication volume must be carefully designed to ensure feature representations are both effective and sufficient. This work studies the dynamic scheduling problem in a multi-region CP scenario, and presents a Timeliness-Aware Multi-region Prioritized (TAMP) scheduling algorithm to trade-off perception accuracy and communication resource usage. Timeliness reflects the utility of information that decays as time elapses, which is manifested by the perception performance in CP tasks. We propose an empirical penalty function that maps the joint impact of Age of Information (AoI) and communication volume to perception performance. Aiming to minimize this timeliness-oriented penalty in the long-term, and recognizing that scheduling decisions have a cumulative effect on subsequent system states, we propose the TAMP scheduling algorithm. TAMP is a Lyapunov-based optimization policy that decomposes the long-term average objective into a per-slot prioritization problem, balancing the scheduling worth against resource cost. We validate our algorithm in both intersection and corridor scenarios with the real-world Roadside Cooperative perception (RCooper) dataset. Extensive simulations demonstrate that TAMP outperforms the best-performing baseline, achieving an Average Precision (AP) improvement of up to 27% across various configurations.
tldr: This paper addresses the problem of dynamic scheduling and resource allocation in multi-region collaborative perception to improve timeliness and accuracy, presenting a Lyapunov-based algorithm (TAMP) that outperforms existing baselines.
tldr_zh: 该论文解决了多区域协同感知中动态调度和资源分配问题，旨在提高及时性和准确性。论文提出了一种基于Lyapunov的算法（TAMP），优于现有基线。
summary_zh: 协同感知（CP）是自动驾驶和智慧城市等应用中的一项关键技术。它涉及传感器之间信息的共享和融合，以克服个体感知的局限性，例如盲点和范围限制。然而，CP面临两个主要挑战。首先，由于环境的动态性，传输信息的时效性对于感知性能至关重要。其次，由于传感器端计算能力有限以及无线带宽受限，通信量必须经过仔细设计，以确保特征表示既有效又充分。本文研究了多区域CP场景中的动态调度问题，并提出了一种时效性感知的多区域优先级（TAMP）调度算法，以权衡感知精度和通信资源的使用。时效性反映了信息随着时间推移而衰减的效用，这体现在CP任务中的感知性能上。我们提出了一个经验惩罚函数，将信息年龄（AoI）和通信量的联合影响映射到感知性能。为了最大限度地减少这种面向时效性的长期惩罚，并认识到调度决策对后续系统状态具有累积效应，我们提出了TAMP调度算法。TAMP是一种基于李雅普诺夫优化的策略，它将长期平均目标分解为每个时隙的优先级问题，从而平衡调度价值和资源成本。我们使用真实世界的路侧协同感知（RCooper）数据集在交叉路口和走廊场景中验证了我们的算法。大量的仿真结果表明，TAMP优于性能最佳的基线，在各种配置下实现了高达27%的平均精度（AP）提升。

title: Phasor Agents: Oscillatory Graphs with Three-Factor Plasticity and Sleep-Staged Learning
summary:
Phasor Agents are dynamical systems whose internal state is a Phasor Graph: a weighted graph of coupled Stuart-Landau oscillators. A Stuart-Landau oscillator is a minimal stable "rhythm generator" (the normal form near a Hopf bifurcation); each oscillator is treated as an abstract computational unit (inspired by, but not claiming to model, biological oscillatory populations). In this interpretation, oscillator phase tracks relative timing (coherence), while amplitude tracks local gain or activity. Relative phase structure serves as a representational medium; coupling weights are learned via three-factor local plasticity - eligibility traces gated by sparse global modulators and oscillation-timed write windows - without backpropagation.
  A central challenge in oscillatory substrates is stability: online weight updates can drive the network into unwanted regimes (e.g., global synchrony), collapsing representational diversity. We therefore separate wake tagging from offline consolidation, inspired by synaptic tagging-and-capture and sleep-stage dynamics: deep-sleep-like gated capture commits tagged changes safely, while REM-like replay reconstructs and perturbs experience for planning.
  A staged experiment suite validates each mechanism with ablations and falsifiers: eligibility traces preserve credit under delayed modulation; compression-progress signals pass timestamp-shuffle controls; phase-coherent retrieval reaches 4x diffusive baselines under noise; wake/sleep separation expands stable learning by 67 percent under matched weight-norm budgets; REM replay improves maze success rate by +45.5 percentage points; and a Tolman-style latent-learning signature - immediate competence and detour advantage after unrewarded exploration, consistent with an internal model - emerges from replay (Tolman, 1948).
  The codebase and all artifacts are open-source.
tldr: The paper introduces 'Phasor Agents,' a novel dynamical system using coupled Stuart-Landau oscillators with three-factor plasticity and sleep-staged learning to address stability challenges in oscillatory substrates, demonstrating improved performance on tasks like maze solving through REM replay.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了“Phasor Agents”，一种新颖的动态系统，它使用耦合的 Stuart-Landau 振荡器以及三因素可塑性和睡眠阶段的学习，来解决振荡基质中的稳定性挑战，并通过 REM 重放证明了其在迷宫求解等任务中的性能提升。
summary_zh:
相位代理（Phasor Agents）是动态系统，其内部状态是一个相位图：一个由耦合的斯图亚特-朗道振荡器组成的加权图。斯图亚特-朗道振荡器是一个最小的稳定“节奏发生器”（霍普夫分岔附近的标准型）；每个振荡器被视为一个抽象的计算单元（灵感来自生物振荡群体，但并非声称对其进行建模）。在这种解释中，振荡器相位追踪相对时间（相干性），而振幅追踪局部增益或活动。相对相位结构充当一种表征媒介；耦合权重通过三因子局部可塑性学习——资格迹通过稀疏的全局调制器和振荡定时写入窗口门控——无需反向传播。

振荡基质的一个核心挑战是稳定性：在线的权重更新可能将网络驱动到不想要的模式（例如，全局同步），导致表征多样性的崩溃。因此，我们受到突触标记和捕获以及睡眠阶段动态的启发，将清醒标记与离线巩固分离：类似深度睡眠的门控捕获安全地提交标记的更改，而类似快速眼动睡眠的重播重建和扰动经验以进行规划。

一个分阶段的实验套件通过消融和证伪验证了每个机制：资格迹在延迟调制下保留信用；压缩进度信号通过时间戳洗牌控制；相位相干检索在噪声下达到扩散基线的4倍；在匹配的权重范数预算下，清醒/睡眠分离将稳定学习扩展了67%；快速眼动睡眠重播将迷宫成功率提高了+45.5个百分点；并且来自重播（Tolman，1948）出现了一个托尔曼式的潜在学习签名——在无奖励探索之后立即具备能力和绕道优势，这与内部模型一致。

代码库和所有工件都是开源的。

title: Lightweight Test-Time Adaptation for EMG-Based Gesture Recognition
summary: Reliable long-term decoding of surface electromyography (EMG) is hindered by signal drift caused by electrode shifts, muscle fatigue, and posture changes. While state-of-the-art models achieve high intra-session accuracy, their performance often degrades sharply. Existing solutions typically demand large datasets or high-compute pipelines that are impractical for energy-efficient wearables. We propose a lightweight framework for Test-Time Adaptation (TTA) using a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) backbone. We introduce three deployment-ready strategies: (i) causal adaptive batch normalization for real-time statistical alignment; (ii) a Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) alignment with experience replay to prevent forgetting; and (iii) meta-learning for rapid, few-shot calibration. Evaluated on the NinaPro DB6 multi-session dataset, our framework significantly bridges the inter-session accuracy gap with minimal overhead. Our results show that experience-replay updates yield superior stability under limited data, while meta-learning achieves competitive performance in one- and two-shot regimes using only a fraction of the data required by current benchmarks. This work establishes a path toward robust, "plug-and-play" myoelectric control for long-term prosthetic use.
tldr: This paper introduces a lightweight test-time adaptation framework for EMG-based gesture recognition to address signal drift, using techniques like adaptive batch normalization, GMM alignment with experience replay, and meta-learning, achieving improved inter-session accuracy with minimal overhead.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种轻量级的肌电信号手势识别测试时自适应框架，通过自适应批归一化、带经验回放的GMM对齐和元学习等技术，解决了信号漂移问题，并以最小的开销提高了会话间准确性。
summary_zh: 表面肌电（EMG）可靠的长期解码受到电极移位、肌肉疲劳和姿势变化引起的信号漂移的阻碍。虽然目前最先进的模型能够实现较高的会话内精度，但它们的性能通常会急剧下降。现有的解决方案通常需要大型数据集或高算力管道，这对于节能型可穿戴设备来说是不切实际的。我们提出了一种轻量级的测试时自适应（TTA）框架，使用时间卷积网络（TCN）作为主干。我们引入了三种可部署的策略：（i）用于实时统计对齐的因果自适应批归一化；（ii）采用经验回放的高斯混合模型（GMM）对齐以防止遗忘；以及（iii）用于快速、少样本校准的元学习。在NinaPro DB6多会话数据集上的评估表明，我们的框架以最小的开销显著缩小了会话间的精度差距。我们的结果表明，经验回放更新在有限数据下产生优异的稳定性，而元学习仅使用当前基准所需数据的一小部分，就能在单次和两次学习体制中实现具有竞争力的性能。这项工作为长期假肢使用的稳健、“即插即用”的肌电控制奠定了基础。

title: Compensation Effect Amplification Control (CEAC): A movement-based approach for coordinated position and velocity control of the elbow of upper-limb prostheses
summary: Despite advances in upper-limb (UL) prosthetic design, achieving intuitive control of intermediate joints - such as the wrist and elbow - remains challenging, particularly for continuous and velocity-modulated movements. We introduce a novel movement-based control paradigm entitled Compensation Effect Amplification Control (CEAC) that leverages users' trunk flexion and extension as input for controlling prosthetic elbow velocity. Considering that the trunk can be both a functional and compensatory joint when performing upper-limb actions, CEAC amplifies the natural coupling between trunk and prosthesis while introducing a controlled delay that allows users to modulate both the position and velocity of the prosthetic joint. We evaluated CEAC in a generic drawing task performed by twelve able-bodied participants using a supernumerary prosthesis with an active elbow. Additionally a multiple-target-reaching task was performed by a subset of ten participants. Results demonstrate task performances comparable to those obtained with natural arm movements, even when gesture velocity or drawing size were varied, while maintaining ergonomic trunk postures. Analysis revealed that CEAC effectively restores joint coordinated action, distributes movement effort between trunk and elbow, enabling intuitive trajectory control without requiring extreme compensatory movements. Overall, CEAC offers a promising control strategy for intermediate joints of UL prostheses, particularly in tasks requiring continuous and precise coordination.
tldr: This paper introduces Compensation Effect Amplification Control (CEAC), a novel method for controlling prosthetic elbow velocity using trunk movements, demonstrating improved performance and intuitive control in drawing and reaching tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为补偿效应放大控制（CEAC）的新方法，该方法利用躯干运动来控制假肢肘部速度，并在绘画和抓取任务中展示了改进的性能和直观的控制。
summary_zh: 尽管上肢（UL）假肢设计取得了进展，但实现对中间关节（如腕关节和肘关节）的直观控制仍然具有挑战性，尤其是在连续且速度调制的运动中。我们引入了一种名为“补偿效应放大控制（CEAC）”的新型基于运动的控制范式，该范式利用用户的躯干屈曲和伸展作为控制假肢肘关节速度的输入。考虑到躯干在进行上肢动作时既可以是功能性关节，也可以是代偿性关节，CEAC放大了躯干和假肢之间的自然耦合，同时引入了可控的延迟，使用户能够调节假肢关节的位置和速度。我们通过一项通用绘图任务评估了CEAC，该任务由十二名健全参与者使用具有主动肘部的超量假肢执行。此外，十名参与者中的一个子集执行了一项多目标到达任务。结果表明，即使在手势速度或绘图尺寸发生变化时，任务表现也能与自然手臂运动的表现相媲美，同时保持符合人体工程学的躯干姿势。分析表明，CEAC有效地恢复了关节协调运动，在躯干和肘关节之间分配了运动负荷，从而实现了直观的轨迹控制，而无需极端的代偿性运动。总体而言，CEAC为上肢假肢的中间关节提供了一种有前景的控制策略，尤其是在需要连续且精确协调的任务中。

title: DAVOS: An Autonomous Vehicle Operating System in the Vehicle Computing Era
summary: Vehicle computing represents a fundamental shift in how autonomous vehicles are designed and deployed, transforming them from isolated transportation systems into mobile computing platforms that support both safety-critical, real-time driving and data-centric services. In this setting, vehicles simultaneously support real-time driving pipelines and a growing set of data-driven applications, placing increased responsibility on the vehicle operating system to coordinate computation, data movement, storage, and access. These demands highlight recurring system considerations related to predictable execution, data and execution protection, efficient handling of high-rate sensor data, and long-term system evolvability, commonly summarized as Safety, Security, Efficiency, and Extensibility (SSEE). Existing vehicle operating systems and runtimes address these concerns in isolation, resulting in fragmented software stacks that limit coordination between autonomy workloads and vehicle data services. This paper presents DAVOS, the Delaware Autonomous Vehicle Operating System, a unified vehicle operating system architecture designed for the vehicle computing context. DAVOS provides a cohesive operating system foundation that supports both real-time autonomy and extensible vehicle computing within a single system framework.
tldr: The paper introduces DAVOS, a unified vehicle operating system designed to support both real-time autonomous driving and data-driven vehicle computing by addressing the SSEE concerns (Safety, Security, Efficiency, and Extensibility).
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DAVOS，一个统一的车辆操作系统，旨在通过解决SSEE (安全、安全、效率和可扩展性) 问题，来支持实时自动驾驶和数据驱动的车辆计算。
summary_zh: 车辆计算代表了自动驾驶汽车设计和部署方式的根本性转变，将它们从孤立的交通运输系统转变为移动计算平台，既支持安全关键的实时驾驶，又支持以数据为中心的服务。在这种背景下，车辆同时支持实时驾驶流程和日益增长的数据驱动型应用程序，这使得车辆操作系统承担了更大的责任，需要协调计算、数据移动、存储和访问。这些需求突出了与可预测执行、数据和执行保护、高效处理高速率传感器数据以及长期系统可演化性相关的反复出现的系统考量，通常概括为安全性、安全性、效率和可扩展性 (SSEE)。现有的车辆操作系统和运行时分别孤立地解决这些问题，导致软件堆栈碎片化，限制了自动驾驶工作负载和车辆数据服务之间的协调。本文介绍了 DAVOS，即特拉华自动驾驶汽车操作系统，这是一种为车辆计算环境设计的统一车辆操作系统架构。 DAVOS 提供了一个有凝聚力的操作系统基础，在一个单一的系统框架内支持实时自动驾驶和可扩展的车载计算。

title: Zero Wrench Control via Wrench Disturbance Observer for Learning-free Peg-in-hole Assembly
summary: This paper proposes a Dynamic Wrench Disturbance Observer (DW-DOB) designed to achieve highly sensitive zero-wrench control in contact-rich manipulation. By embedding task-space inertia into the observer nominal model, DW-DOB cleanly separates intrinsic dynamic reactions from true external wrenches. This preserves sensitivity to small forces and moments while ensuring robust regulation of contact wrenches. A passivity-based analysis further demonstrates that DW-DOB guarantees stable interactions under dynamic conditions, addressing the shortcomings of conventional observers that fail to compensate for inertial effects. Peg-in-hole experiments at industrial tolerances (H7/h6) validate the approach, yielding deeper and more compliant insertions with minimal residual wrenches and outperforming a conventional wrench disturbance observer and a PD baseline. These results highlight DW-DOB as a practical learning-free solution for high-precision zero-wrench control in contact-rich tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces a Dynamic Wrench Disturbance Observer (DW-DOB) for precise zero-wrench control in contact-rich manipulation tasks like peg-in-hole assembly, demonstrating improved performance over existing methods without requiring learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种动态力/力矩扰动观测器（DW-DOB），用于在诸如销钉孔装配等接触丰富的操作任务中实现精确的零力/力矩控制，展示了优于现有方法且无需学习的性能。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种动态力/力矩扰动观测器(DW-DOB), 旨在实现接触丰富的操作中高灵敏度的零力/力矩控制。通过将任务空间惯性嵌入观测器的标称模型中，DW-DOB清晰地分离了固有动态反作用力与真正的外部力/力矩。这既保留了对微小作用力/力矩的灵敏度，又确保了对接触力/力矩的鲁棒调节。基于无源性的分析进一步表明，DW-DOB保证了动态条件下的稳定交互，解决了传统观测器无法补偿惯性效应的缺点。在工业公差标准(H7/h6)下的销钉插入孔实验验证了该方法，实现了更深且更顺滑的插入，具有最小的残余力/力矩，并且优于传统的力/力矩扰动观测器和PD基线方法。这些结果表明，DW-DOB是一种实用的、无需学习的解决方案，适用于接触丰富的任务中的高精度零力/力矩控制。

title: Discrete Fourier Transform-based Point Cloud Compression for Efficient SLAM in Featureless Terrain
summary: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is an essential technology for the efficiency and reliability of unmanned robotic exploration missions. While the onboard computational capability and communication bandwidth are critically limited, the point cloud data handled by SLAM is large in size, attracting attention to data compression methods. To address such a problem, in this paper, we propose a new method for compressing point cloud maps by exploiting the Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT). The proposed technique converts the Digital Elevation Model (DEM) to the frequency-domain 2D image and omits its high-frequency components, focusing on the exploration of gradual terrains such as planets and deserts. Unlike terrains with detailed structures such as artificial environments, high-frequency components contribute little to the representation of gradual terrains. Thus, this method is effective in compressing data size without significant degradation of the point cloud. We evaluated the method in terms of compression rate and accuracy using camera sequences of two terrains with different elevation profiles.
tldr: This paper introduces a point cloud compression method based on Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) for efficient SLAM in featureless terrains, applicable to resource-constrained robotic exploration.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于离散傅里叶变换（DFT）的点云压缩方法，用于在无特征地形中实现高效的SLAM，适用于资源受限的机器人探索。
summary_zh: 同时定位与地图构建（SLAM）是无人机器人探索任务效率和可靠性的关键技术。 尽管机载计算能力和通信带宽受到严格限制，但SLAM处理的点云数据规模庞大，这使得数据压缩方法备受关注。 为了解决这个问题，本文提出了一种利用离散傅里叶变换（DFT）压缩点云地图的新方法。 该技术将数字高程模型（DEM）转换为频域2D图像，并忽略其高频成分，专注于探索行星和沙漠等渐变地形。 与人工环境等具有精细结构的地形不同，高频成分对渐变地形的表示贡献较小。 因此，该方法能够有效地压缩数据大小，而不会显著降低点云的质量。 我们使用具有不同高程剖面的两种地形的相机序列，从压缩率和精度方面评估了该方法。

title: Combining facial videos and biosignals for stress estimation during driving
summary: Reliable stress recognition from facial videos is challenging due to stress's subjective nature and voluntary facial control. While most methods rely on Facial Action Units, the role of disentangled 3D facial geometry remains underexplored. We address this by analyzing stress during distracted driving using EMOCA-derived 3D expression and pose coefficients. Paired hypothesis tests between baseline and stressor phases reveal that 41 of 56 coefficients show consistent, phase-specific stress responses comparable to physiological markers. Building on this, we propose a Transformer-based temporal modeling framework and assess unimodal, early-fusion, and cross-modal attention strategies. Cross-Modal Attention fusion of EMOCA and physiological signals achieves best performance (AUROC 92\%, Accuracy 86.7\%), with EMOCA-gaze fusion also competitive (AUROC 91.8\%). This highlights the effectiveness of temporal modeling and cross-modal attention for stress recognition.
tldr: This paper explores stress recognition during distracted driving by analyzing 3D facial geometry derived from facial videos and physiological signals, achieving high accuracy through a Transformer-based temporal modeling framework with cross-modal attention.
tldr_zh: 本文通过分析面部视频和生理信号导出的3D面部几何结构，探索了在分心驾驶期间的压力识别，通过基于Transformer的时间建模框架和跨模态注意力实现了高精度。
summary_zh: 从面部视频中可靠地识别压力具有挑战性，这是由于压力的主观性和自愿的面部控制。尽管大多数方法依赖于面部动作单元，但解耦的3D面部几何结构的作用仍未得到充分探索。我们通过分析分散注意力驾驶期间的压力来解决这个问题，方法是使用EMOCA导出的3D表情和姿态系数。基线阶段和压力源阶段之间的配对假设检验表明，56个系数中有41个表现出与生理指标相当的始终如一的、阶段特异性的压力反应。在此基础上，我们提出了一个基于Transformer的时间建模框架，并评估了单模态、早期融合和跨模态注意力策略。EMOCA和生理信号的跨模态注意力融合实现了最佳性能（AUROC 92\%，准确率 86.7\%），而EMOCA-注视融合也具有竞争力（AUROC 91.8\%）。这突显了时间建模和跨模态注意力对压力识别的有效性。
